---
title: "TITLE OF THE EXPLAINER"
description: "One-sentence hook that appears on the Library card and as the social preview."
author: "Kader Mohideen"
date: "YYYY-MM-DD"          # publish date — controls ordering in the Library
categories: [Topic, Tag]    # shows as a filter chip on the Library page
image: ../banners/SLUG.png  # 1200x630 card image (optional but recommended)
reading-time: true
toc: true
toc-location: left
toc-title: "On this page"
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <link rel="stylesheet" href="/styles/explainer.css">
# If you want interactive demos, also uncomment the next two lines:
# include-after-body:
#   - text: |
#       <script src="/styles/ml-viz.js"></script>
---

::: {.explainer-body}

![](../banners/SLUG.png){.xpl-fig}

::: {.xpl-lead}
Open with a punchy one-paragraph hook — the question this chapter answers, in plain language. This is the part that makes someone keep reading.
:::

## The short version

A two or three sentence answer for the impatient reader. Then the rest of the page earns it.

::: {.xpl-key}
**Key idea:** State the single most important takeaway in one bold line. Readers should be able to skim just these boxes and still get the gist.
:::

## How it actually works

Explain the mechanism step by step. Keep paragraphs short. Drop a diagram whenever a picture beats words:

::: {.xpl-fig}
![](resources/diagram-1.png)
<figcaption>Caption describing what the diagram shows.</figcaption>
:::

## A worked example

Walk through one concrete example with real numbers. Code blocks render with syntax highlighting:

```python
# example code
print("hello")
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Use callouts (`note`, `tip`, `warning`, `important`) for asides, gotchas, and pro-tips.
:::

<!-- OPTIONAL interactive demo (needs the ml-viz.js include above):
::: {.xpl-try}
<div class="ml-viz" data-viz="vecadd" data-params="{}"></div>
:::
-->

## Why it matters

Tie it back to the real world — where this shows up, why it's worth understanding.

## Going deeper

- [Reference or paper](https://example.com)
- [Another resource](https://example.com)

::: {.xpl-nav}
[← Back to Library](../../kader-library.html)
[All explainers →](../../kader-library.html)
:::

:::
